name: hcportal_bulkAddHashers
description: Bulk adds or updates multiple hashers (members) for a kennel. Accepts a JSON array of hasher data, validates email format, creates new HC.Hasher records for new users, creates or updates HC.HasherKennelMap relationships, and optionally updates historical run counts. Uses a cursor to iterate through the input array. All writes are wrapped in a single transaction.

parameters.name: publicHasherId
parameters.description: Auth: public hasher ID of the authenticated user performing the bulk add. Validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth.

parameters.name: accessToken
parameters.description: Auth: short-lived cryptographic token. Validated via HC6.ValidatePortalAuth with @publicKennelId as the paramString.

parameters.name: publicKennelId
parameters.description: Auth/routing: public kennel ID for which hashers are being bulk added. Required. Used as paramString for auth token validation.

parameters.name: newHasherJson
parameters.description: JSON array of hasher objects to add/update. Each object may contain: publicHasherId, publicKennelId, firstName, lastName, eMail, hashName, historicTotalRuns, historicHaring, addHasherStatus. Must be non-NULL and at least 5 characters (LEN check). Parsed via OPENJSON with explicit schema.

rowsets.name: ErrorEnvelope
rowsets.description: Returned on any validation, auth, or runtime error. Short-circuits on first error.

rowsets.name: BulkAddResult
rowsets.description: Returned on success. Contains one row per input hasher with updated publicHasherId and addHasherStatus reflecting the outcome. Possible addHasherStatus values: 'NEW HC USER' (new Hasher record created), 'NEW MEMBER' (new HasherKennelMap record created or existing inactive member reactivated), 'UPDATE RUN COUNTS' (historical counts updated), 'NO CHANGE' (already a current member with no changes needed), 'ERROR' (invalid email format, row not processed).

columns.name: publicHasherId
columns.description: The public hasher ID — newly generated GUID for new users, or NULL/passthrough for invalid-email rows

columns.name: publicKennelId
columns.description: The public kennel ID from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: firstName
columns.description: First name from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: lastName
columns.description: Last name from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: email
columns.description: Email address from the JSON input (passthrough). Used as the unique key for hasher lookup.

columns.name: hashName
columns.description: Hash name from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: historicTotalRuns
columns.description: Historical total run count from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: historicHaring
columns.description: Historical haring count from the JSON input (passthrough)

columns.name: addHasherStatus
columns.description: Processing outcome per row. Values: 'NEW HC USER' (Hasher record created), 'NEW MEMBER' (HasherKennelMap created or reactivated), 'UPDATE RUN COUNTS' (historical counts changed), 'NO CHANGE' (no action needed), 'ERROR' (invalid email, skipped)

sideEffects: INSERT into HC.Hasher (PublicHasherId, FirstName, LastName, HashName, Email, HomeKennelId, Photo) for each input row with a valid email and no existing Hasher record
INSERT into HC.HasherKennelMap (UserId, KennelId, Following, MembershipExpirationDate) with sentinel '2999-12-31' for new kennel memberships
UPDATE HC.HasherKennelMap (Following, MembershipExpirationDate) to Following=1 and '2999-12-31' for existing members who are no longer active
UPDATE HC.HasherKennelMap (HistoricalTotalRunCount, updatedAt) if the input value differs from the stored value
UPDATE HC.HasherKennelMap (HistoricalHaringCount, updatedAt) if the input value differs from the stored value
EXEC HC.nonApi_updateRunCountsByUser for each row where historical run counts are updated (recalculates aggregate run counts)
breakingChangeRules: Never remove a column from any rowset
Never change a sentinel value meaning
Never change the addHasherStatus string values ('NEW HC USER', 'NEW MEMBER', 'UPDATE RUN COUNTS', 'NO CHANGE', 'ERROR') without updating all portal parsing logic
Never change the '2999-12-31' never-expires sentinel date without updating all callers
